title: Roasted Cauliflower and Tomato Salad
excerpt: Ingredients ½ head cauliflower, broken into florets 1 whole bulb fennel, cut into wedges 2 onions, cut into wedges 200 g baby tomatoes, halved Olive oil 15 ml tomato paste 65 ml prepared vegetable or chicken stock 30 ml white vinegar 90 ml olive oil 65 ml spring onions (the green part), sliced 1 large, …

seo.title: Roasted Cauliflower and Tomato Salad - Marinda Kook

html:
Ingredients

  • ½ head cauliflower, broken into florets
  • 1 whole bulb fennel, cut into wedges
  • 2 onions, cut into wedges
  • 200 g baby tomatoes, halved
  • Olive oil
  • 15 ml tomato paste
  • 65 ml prepared vegetable or chicken stock
  • 30 ml white vinegar
  • 90 ml olive oil
  • 65 ml spring onions (the green part), sliced
  • 1 large, ripe tomato, skin and seeds removed and cut into small cubes
  • Sugar, salt and coarsely ground black pepper
  • Extra olive oil
  • Rocket, roughly torn (optional)

Method

Place the cauliflower, fennel, onions and tomatoes in a mixing bowl and drizzle with olive oil. Toss gently. Season to taste with salt and pepper and spread out evenly on a baking tray. Roast on high heat for a few minutes until dark speckles start appearing here and there. Remove from the oven and set aside to cool to room temperature.

Meanwhile, make the salad dressing: Mix the tomato paste, stock and vinegar together and season to taste. Whisk in the olive oil, then stir in the spring onions and tomato cubes. Season to taste with sugar, salt and pepper.

Just before serving, gently mix the cauliflower mixture with the rocket (if using), place in a salad bowl and drizzle with the dressing, or serve the dressing on the side.

Makes 6 generous portions to go with any main course.

Easy!
